title: Flavonoid Profiling by Liquid Chromatography Coupled to Mass Spectrometry
author: Camilo E. Vital
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zggf3tw

steps.title: Collect samples of plant tissues, immediately...
steps.instruction: Collect samples of plant tissues, immediately freeze in liquid nitrogen and store them in freezer -80° C until use.

steps.title: Macerate the samples in liquid nitrogen using mortar and pestle
steps.instruction: Macerate the samples in liquid nitrogen using mortar and pestle. Do not allow to thaw. Weigh approximately 100mg of each sample into microtubes (2ml) and annotate the weight (used for absolute quantifications)

steps.title: Add 200 μl of a solution...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of a solution containing 75% methanol and 0.1% formic acid.

steps.title: Sonicate the samples for 30 minutes...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the samples for 30 minutes and centrifuge for 14000g for 10 minutes at 4 o C.

steps.title: Collect the supernatants in new tubes
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatants in new tubes. To the remaining pellet, repeat the procedures 3 and 4 (supernatant

steps.title: and then pool the supernatants
steps.instruction: and then pool the supernatants.

steps.title: Filter the supernatant using disposable 0
steps.instruction: Filter the supernatant using disposable 0.2 ml PVDF membrane.

steps.title: Dry the methanolic extracts in speed...
steps.instruction: Dry the methanolic extracts in speed vac and resuspend in deionized water (50uL).

steps.title: Store the samples in freezer -80ºC...
steps.instruction: Store the samples in freezer -80ºC until analyze them using LC-MS.

steps.title: Analysis of target phenolic compounds (Table 1)
steps.instruction: Analysis of target phenolic compounds (Table 1) . 2 ) Analysis of flavonoid classes using a non-target method with insource fragmentation ISCID ( Table 2 ), following Abrankó and Szilvássy (2015).

steps.title: Determination of glycoconjugates flavonoid m/z values...
steps.instruction: Determination of glycoconjugates flavonoid m/z values and putative structures using precursor ions. Table

steps.title: Transition list and MS parameters used...
steps.instruction: Transition list and MS parameters used for analysis of twenty-one target phenolic compounds %s %s Table

steps.title: LC/MS conditions to analyze the flavonoid...
steps.instruction: LC/MS conditions to analyze the flavonoid classes and used as input in the Skyline analysis. %s %s

steps.title: UHPLC system containing vials for 50...
steps.instruction: UHPLC system containing vials for 50 μl and loop for injection of 5 μl.

steps.title: Use a mass spectrometer triple quadrupole...
steps.instruction: Use a mass spectrometer triple quadrupole that enable product, precursor and MRM scans. The methods were optimized for an Ultra-High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (UHPLC) coupled online to a mass spectrometer QQQ (triple quadrupole).

steps.title: Chromatographic separation is performed by reverse...
steps.instruction: Chromatographic separation is performed by reverse phase columns, such as an analytical Zorbax Eclipse Plus C18 (1.8 μm, 2.1 x 50mm) and a guard column Zorbax SB-C18, 1.8 μm.

steps.title: The mobile phase consists of buffers...
steps.instruction: The mobile phase consists of buffers A (water acetic acid 0.02%) and B (acetonitrile acetic acid 0.02%) and a gradient of %B: 5% x 0 min­ -1 ; 60% x 11 min­ -1 ; 95% x 13 min­ -1 ; 95% x 17 min­ -1 ; 5% x 19 min­ -1 ; and 5% x 20 min­ -1 . The solvent flow rate is 0.3ml x min­ -1 in a column at 30°C. The mass spectrometer is operated by positive mode according to method for flavonoids detection.

steps.title: The ionization method used in the...
steps.instruction: The ionization method used in the mass spectrometry was an ESI (Electrospray Ionization) under the conditions: gas temperature of 300 °C, nitrogen flow rate of 10 L x min­ -1 , nebulizer pressure of 35 psi and capillary voltage of 4000 V.

steps.title: Prepare a standard solution containing 1
steps.instruction: Prepare a standard solution containing 1.0 ug/mL of each compound in methanol 50% and transfer it to vials. (foi usado essa concetração para injetar neste momento?).

steps.title: Setup a product ion scan method...
steps.instruction: Setup a product ion scan method for each compound and optimize the transmission and fragmentation parameters. This procedure may be executed manually or automatically.

steps.title: Select the higher intensity fragment ions...
steps.instruction: Select the higher intensity fragment ions to compose the transition list used in the scan mode by MRM (Multiple Reaction Monitoring) as illustrated in the Table

steps.title: Prepare serial dilutions of 0
steps.instruction: Prepare serial dilutions of 0.1 ng/mL up to 1.0 ng/mL in according with the mass spectrometer sensitivity and linearity. Two replicate is enough to prepare the standard curves.

steps.title: Inject 5
steps.instruction: Inject 5.0 ul and perform the MRM method as setup in

steps.title: Generate the area from XICs (extracted...
steps.instruction: Generate the area from XICs (extracted ion chromatograms) for each dilution using Skyline software. Note: A complete tutorial for processing of the mass spectra data using Skyline software is described in the Supplementary Material

steps.title: Export the XICs to Microsoft Excel...
steps.instruction: Export the XICs to Microsoft Excel following the instructions of the Skyline tutorial (Supplementary Material 1).

steps.title: Prepare the standard curves for each...
steps.instruction: Prepare the standard curves for each compound in ng/mL of fresh tissue. Use the XIC area versus flavonoid concentration (ng/mL) to generated linear curves ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Schematic diagram of the steps used...
steps.instruction: Schematic diagram of the steps used for prepare of the standard curves.

steps.title: Inject all sample randomly using the...
steps.instruction: Inject all sample randomly using the MRM method, which was used to generate the standard curves. Use the raw data to analyze mass spectra using Skyline software (section 3.3; step 6).

steps.title: For each compound, use the XICs...
steps.instruction: For each compound, use the XICs area exported to Microsoft Excel and convert to ng/mL using the standard linear curves (section 3.3; step 6). Quantification of Kaempferol for a Sample 1 with a XIC area of

steps.title: Kaempferol ; transition 287 >153; retention time 8
steps.instruction: Kaempferol ; transition 287 >153; retention time 8.6 (Table 1). Conversion to ng/mL: Standard curve for Kaempherol: y=13.524x + 1564.6; where x is the sample concentration (ng/mL) and y is the XIC area (arbitrary units) Kaempherol concentration in the sample

steps.title: 420.4 ng/mL Conversion of ng/mL to ng/g of fresh tissue
steps.instruction: 420.4 ng/mL Conversion of ng/mL to ng/g of fresh tissue: 420,4 ---- 1000ul x------ 50ul Then:21 ng of Kaempherol from 100mg of fresh tissue, 210 ng/g fresh leaves

steps.title: Inject in LC/MS system the standards...
steps.instruction: Inject in LC/MS system the standards of glycoconjugates, such as rutin, hesperidin, naringin to optimize the in-source energy for better release the aglycone core before the first quadrupole.

steps.title: Inject also in the LC/MS system...
steps.instruction: Inject also in the LC/MS system the standards (such as dadzein, apigenin, genistein, etc) for each aglycone belonging to the flavonoid classes. Use the product ion scan to optimize the fragmentation conditions to generate the fragment ions. Do not use high energy in the source,just use the optimal transmission setting. Verify the fragments which show intensities distinct from aglycones of the same class (same nominal mass, isomers), as reference see Abrankó and Szilvássy (

steps.title: and Gómez et al
steps.instruction: and Gómez et al. (

steps.title: prepare a MRM method ( Table 2 )
steps.instruction: prepare a MRM method ( Table 2 ).

steps.title: Inject again all the standards using...
steps.instruction: Inject again all the standards using MRM scan for the selected transitions in the

steps.title: Use four transitions by compound (Table 2)
steps.instruction: Use four transitions by compound (Table 2). Use the raw spectrum as input of Skyline software and export the XICs areas to generate the FRI%, which will used as signature for identification of each compound from each class using the Microsoft Excel. For instance, despite kaempferol and luteolin show the same nominal mass of m/z 287, the FRI% of fragments (153, 135, 121 and

steps.title: enable a undoubted differentiation of the...
steps.instruction: enable a undoubted differentiation of the aglycone ( Figure 2 ). Figure

steps.title: Fragment relative intensity (FRI%) from Kaempferol and Luteolin:
steps.instruction: Fragment relative intensity (FRI%) from Kaempferol and Luteolin:

steps.title: 1) Inject all sample randomly in...
steps.instruction: 1) Inject all sample randomly in LC/MS system using the in-source/MRM setup ( section 3.4 ) 2) . Use the raw spectra as input of Skyline software to obtain the XIC area for each transition ( Figure 3A ) and the FRI% for each retention time (RT) ( Figure 3B ). Note: The eluted compounds that shown the same FRI% of the specific standard will be considerate as containing the same aglycone ( Figure 3B ). Figure 3. Nontarget analysis of flavonoids. Analysis of Apigenin and genistein results in the Skyline software ( A ) and fragment relative intensity (FRI %) in the standard and plant sample ( B ). Compound with RT 6.7 and 7.2 are characteristic of apigenin core.

steps.title: Inject again the all sample randomly in the LC/MS system
steps.instruction: Inject again the all sample randomly in the LC/MS system. Setup a precursor ion scan for each class using the same parameters of the section 3.

steps.title: Use the mass spectrometermanufacturer software to...
steps.instruction: Use the mass spectrometermanufacturer software to proces the spectrum raw data. The Figure 4 shows the layout of Mass Hunter software (Agilent). For each XIC, annotate the m/z values of the precursor ions eluted in each RT.

steps.title: Search each m/z value (deprotonated value, subtracted of 1
steps.instruction: Search each m/z value (deprotonated value, subtracted of 1.0 Da)in a mass spectrum database, such as MassBank (https://massbank.eu/MassBank/), by “Quick Source module” using the mass tolerance of 0.3 Da. Additionally, search for scientific reports which describe flavonoids with the same nominal mass containing the same aglycone core. Then subtract the nominal mass of glycoconjugate of the aglycone core to obtain the sugar moiety. Figure 4 . Determination of the m/z values for the detected Glucoconjugated flavonoids in the step 3.5.

steps.title: Install the Skyline Package (32 or 64 bits)
steps.instruction: Install the Skyline Package (32 or 64 bits): https://skyline.ms/wiki/home/software/Skyline/page.view?name=SkylineInstall_64_4-1&submit=false or https://skyline.ms/project/home/software/Skyline/begin.view

steps.title: Generate a transition list table in...
steps.instruction: Generate a transition list table in accordance with the MRM parameters such as in the Table 1 .

steps.title: Open the transition list file in...
steps.instruction: Open the transition list file in the OpenOffice software (Avoid language incompatibly). Select and Copy all lines, except the column heading.

steps.title: Open Skyline package and click in blank document
steps.instruction: Open Skyline package and click in blank document

steps.title: Proceed edit >>>> insert >>>> transition list
steps.instruction: Proceed edit >>>> insert >>>> transition list

steps.title: Click in “ columns” to edit
steps.instruction: Click in “ columns” to edit . Select in accordance with the transition list create before . Use the mouse to dragging and to change the column order.

steps.title: Paste the transition list information
steps.instruction: Paste the transition list information. Click in check for errors

steps.title: Click in “ insert”
steps.instruction: Click in “ insert” .

steps.title: Open Setting >>> transitions setting and...
steps.instruction: Open Setting >>> transitions setting and configure to processing the raw data from the QqQ mass spectrometry

steps.title: Open Setting >>> Save Current
steps.instruction: Open Setting >>> Save Current .

steps.title: Open File >> import >> Results
steps.instruction: Open File >> import >> Results

steps.title: Click in Ok to import and...
steps.instruction: Click in Ok to import and save the Project as myprojectname . Sky

steps.title: In the windown importResults >> click in OK
steps.instruction: In the windown importResults >> click in OK !! Select and import all data from files .d, generated by LC/MS QqQ for all the samples.

steps.title: After the data uploading, open View...
steps.instruction: After the data uploading, open View >>>> retention times >>> and select ReplicateComparison

steps.title: Open View >>>> Peak Areas >>> and select replicate comparison
steps.instruction: Open View >>>> Peak Areas >>> and select replicate comparison

steps.title: Proceed a double-click in the tabs...
steps.instruction: Proceed a double-click in the tabs “Peak Areas” and “Retention Times” .

steps.title: Click over the retention time (RT)...
steps.instruction: Click over the retention time (RT) bar to edit and correct the selected chromatogram area that was generated automatically. The dashed line could be moved!! Repeat for all compound and samples!!!

steps.title: Save the project
steps.instruction: Save the project.

steps.title: Open File >>> Export >> report...
steps.instruction: Open File >>> Export >> report >> select transition result >>>> OK, to export the peak areas of the all XICs as spreadsheet file. Open this file in the OpenOffice and use the XIC area for each compounds to obtain the quantitative information.
